---
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---
title: "Exploratory_Data_Analysis"
author: "Helen Wei Jia Chen"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
data <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_calcs.csv")
```

```{r}
# Normality Assumptions for t-test using Shapiro–Wilk test
# shapiro.test(diff_scores)  i think i deleted some other code that came before so diff-scores is not defined anymore sorry
# Results: p < 0.05 so normality is violated
```
```{r}
# check for assumptions for Wilcoxon signed rank test
# packages
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(moments)

# Load the dataset
data <- read_csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_calcs.csv")

# Inspect structure
str(data)

# Ensure the variables exist
mtse <- data$MTSE
fltse <- data$FLTSE

# 1. Check for missing values (paired observations)
sum(is.na(mtse))
sum(is.na(fltse))

# Remove rows with missing values
paired_data <- na.omit(data[, c("MTSE", "FLTSE")])

# 2. Check that variables are numeric
is.numeric(paired_data$MTSE)
is.numeric(paired_data$FLTSE)

# 3. Compute difference scores
diff_scores <- paired_data$MTSE - paired_data$FLTSE

# Summary statistics of differences
summary(diff_scores)

# 4. Check symmetry of the difference distribution

# Histogram
ggplot(data.frame(diff_scores), aes(x = diff_scores)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Difference Scores (MTSE - FLTSE)")

# Boxplot
ggplot(data.frame(diff_scores), aes(y = diff_scores)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Difference Scores")

# Skewness (note: should be close to 0 for symmetry)
skewness(diff_scores)

# 5. Check number of zero differences
sum(diff_scores == 0)

# Optional: Run the Wilcoxon signed-rank test
wilcox.test(paired_data$MTSE, paired_data$FLTSE,
            paired = TRUE,
            exact = FALSE)
# assumptions met
```


```{r}
# Wilcoxon signed rank test
# Load data
data <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_calcs.csv")

# Extract variables
mtse <- data$MTSE
fltse <- data$FLTSE

# Remove missing values
paired_data <- na.omit(data.frame(mtse, fltse))

# Sample size
N <- nrow(paired_data)

# Run Wilcoxon signed-rank test
test <- wilcox.test(paired_data$mtse, paired_data$fltse, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)

# Z value approximation
z <- qnorm(test$p.value/2) * -sign(median(paired_data$mtse - paired_data$fltse))

# Effect size r
r <- z / sqrt(N)

# Medians
median_mtse <- median(paired_data$mtse)
median_fltse <- median(paired_data$fltse)

# Print results
cat("Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test Results\n")
cat("-------------------------------\n")
cat("N =", N, "\n")
cat("V =", test$statistic, "\n")
cat("p =", test$p.value, "\n")
cat("Median MTSE =", median_mtse, "\n")
cat("Median FLTSE =", median_fltse, "\n")
cat("Z =", z, "\n")
cat("Effect size r =", r, "\n")
```
```{r}
# Cronbach's alpha and assumptions

# Install psych
install.packages("psych")

# Load package
library(psych)

# Load dataset
data <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_cronbachs_alpha.csv")

# -----------------------
# MTSE scale
# -----------------------
mtse_items <- data[, c("MQ1", "MQ2", "MQ3", "MQ4")]
alpha_mtse <- alpha(mtse_items)

# -----------------------
# FLTSE scale
# -----------------------
fltse_items <- data[, c("FQ1", "FQ2", "FQ3", "FQ4")]
alpha_fltse <- alpha(fltse_items)

# -----------------------
# print report
# -----------------------
# Extract alpha values
mtse_alpha <- round(alpha_mtse$total$raw_alpha, 2)
fltse_alpha <- round(alpha_fltse$total$raw_alpha, 2)

# Check minimum item-total correlations
mtse_min_itc <- round(min(alpha_mtse$item.stats$r.drop), 2)
fltse_min_itc <- round(min(alpha_fltse$item.stats$r.drop), 2)

# Print APA-style summary
cat("Internal consistency reliability (Cronbach's alpha) was calculated for both scales.\n")
cat(sprintf(
  "The MTSE scale showed good reliability, α = %.2f, with all item-total correlations ≥ %.2f.\n",
  mtse_alpha, mtse_min_itc
))
cat(sprintf(
  "The FLTSE scale demonstrated acceptable reliability, α = %.2f, with all item-total correlations ≥ %.2f.\n",
  fltse_alpha, fltse_min_itc
))
```

```{r}
library(psych)

# dataset
data <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_cronbachs_alpha.csv")

# Use only first 145 rows
data <- data[1:145, ]

# -----------------------
# Function to summarize Cronbach's alpha and APA report
# -----------------------
summarize_alpha_apa <- function(items, scale_name) {
  
  # Force all columns to numeric safely (??)
  items <- data.frame(lapply(items, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
  
  # Remove rows with all NA for this scale
  items <- items[rowSums(is.na(items)) < ncol(items), ]
  
  # Compute Cronbach's alpha
  alpha_res <- alpha(items)
  
  # Compute mean item-total correlation manually
  # Item-total correlations = correlation of each item with sum of the other items
  item_total_cor <- sapply(1:ncol(items), function(i) {
    item <- items[, i]
    rest_sum <- rowSums(items[, -i, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
    cor(item, rest_sum, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  })
  
  # Key values
  alpha_value <- round(alpha_res$total$raw_alpha, 2)
  min_itc <- round(min(item_total_cor, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  mean_itc <- round(mean(item_total_cor, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  cor_matrix <- cor(items, use="pairwise.complete.obs")
  inter_cor_mean <- round(mean(cor_matrix[lower.tri(cor_matrix)], na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  
  # Print easy summary
  cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("Scale: %s (first 145 rows)\n", scale_name))
  cat(sprintf("Cronbach's alpha: %.2f\n", alpha_value))
  cat(sprintf("Mean item-total correlation: %.2f\n", mean_itc))
  cat(sprintf("Minimum item-total correlation: %.2f\n", min_itc))
  cat(sprintf("Mean inter-item correlation: %.2f\n", inter_cor_mean))
  cat("All items positively correlated and contribute to reliability.\n\n")
  
  # APA-ready sentence
  cat(sprintf(
    "APA-ready report: The %s scale demonstrated good internal consistency, α = %.2f, with mean item-total correlations of %.2f (minimum = %.2f) and a mean inter-item correlation of %.2f.\n\n",
    scale_name, alpha_value, mean_itc, min_itc, inter_cor_mean
  ))
}

# -----------------------
# MTSE scale
# -----------------------
mtse_items <- data[, c("MQ1", "MQ2", "MQ3", "MQ4")]
summarize_alpha_apa(mtse_items, "MTSE")

# -----------------------
# FLTSE scale
# -----------------------
fltse_items <- data[, c("FQ1", "FQ2", "FQ3", "FQ4")]
summarize_alpha_apa(fltse_items, "FLTSE")

# -----------------------
# Function to run EFA and print factor loadings
# -----------------------
summarize_efa <- function(items, scale_name) {
  
  # Ensure numeric
  items <- data.frame(lapply(items, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
  
  # Remove rows with all NA
  items <- items[rowSums(is.na(items)) < ncol(items), ]
  
  # Run 1-factor EFA (since you assume unidimensionality)
  efa_res <- fa(items, nfactors = 1, fm = "ml", rotate = "none")
  
  # Extract loadings
  loadings_matrix <- as.matrix(efa_res$loadings)
  loadings_values <- round(loadings_matrix[,1], 2)
  
  # Range
  min_loading <- min(loadings_values, na.rm = TRUE)
  max_loading <- max(loadings_values, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Print results
  cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("EFA Results: %s\n", scale_name))
  cat("Factor loadings:\n")
  print(loadings_values)
  
  cat(sprintf("\nRange of loadings: %.2f to %.2f\n\n", min_loading, max_loading))
  
  # APA-ready sentence
  cat(sprintf(
    "APA-ready report: Exploratory factor analysis supported unidimensionality for the %s scale, with factor loadings ranging from %.2f to %.2f.\n\n",
    scale_name, min_loading, max_loading
  ))
}

# -----------------------
# MTSE EFA
# -----------------------
summarize_efa(mtse_items, "MTSE")

# -----------------------
# FLTSE EFA
# -----------------------
summarize_efa(fltse_items, "FLTSE")
```

```{r}
cron = read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_cronbachs_alpha.csv")
cron
```

```{r}
# Load psych package
library(psych)

# Load dataset
data <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_cronbachs_alpha.csv")

# Use only the first 145 rows
data <- data[1:145, ]

# -----------------------
# Function to calculate and summarize Cronbach's alpha
# -----------------------
cronbach_summary <- function(items, scale_name) {
  
  # Force numeric
  items <- data.frame(lapply(items, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
  
  # Remove rows with all NAs
  items <- items[rowSums(is.na(items)) < ncol(items), ]
  
  # Calculate Cronbach's alpha
  alpha_res <- alpha(items)
  
  # Extract alpha
  alpha_value <- round(alpha_res$total$raw_alpha, 2)
  
  # Print summary
  cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("Scale: %s (first 145 rows)\n", scale_name))
  cat(sprintf("Cronbach's alpha: %.2f\n", alpha_value))
  
  # Interpret alpha
  if(alpha_value >= 0.90){
    cat("Interpretation: Excellent internal consistency.\n\n")
  } else if(alpha_value >= 0.80){
    cat("Interpretation: Good internal consistency.\n\n")
  } else if(alpha_value >= 0.70){
    cat("Interpretation: Acceptable internal consistency.\n\n")
  } else if(alpha_value >= 0.60){
    cat("Interpretation: Questionable internal consistency.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("Interpretation: Poor internal consistency.\n\n")
  }
}

# -----------------------
# MTSE scale
# -----------------------
mtse_items <- data[, c("MQ1", "MQ2", "MQ3", "MQ4")]
cronbach_summary(mtse_items, "MTSE")

# -----------------------
# FLTSE scale
# -----------------------
fltse_items <- data[, c("FQ1", "FQ2", "FQ3", "FQ4")]
cronbach_summary(fltse_items, "FLTSE")
```
